title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: Guidance future Claude (human) sessions working cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison.

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: cpaic extends component network meta-analysis (cNMA) population-adjusted indirect comparison (PAIC) methods (STC, MAIC, ML-NMR). goal take treatment network disconnected (two sub-networks common comparator) : Reconnect using additive component structure cNMA — sub-networks share treatment components, component effects bridge gap (Rücker et al. 2020/2021). Adjust bridged comparisons -study imbalance effect modifiers using anchored STC, MAIC, ML-NMR, individual patient data (IPD) available. result indirect comparison connected population-adjusted. existing package ; novel methodology. See documentation/refs/ literature builds documentation/PROGRESS.md running build log.

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: Connection layer = additive cNMA. Design decomposition delta = X beta = B C beta, B edge-incidence (contrast) matrix C treatment--component matrix. netmeta::discomb() estimates component effects across disconnected network weighted least squares beta_hat = (X' W X)^+ X' W d, Cov(theta_hat) = C (X' W X)^+ C'. Identifiable iff rank(X) = n_components. Adjustment layer = PAIC. IPD-bearing edge corrected effect-modifier imbalance, producing population-adjusted contrast d variance feed connection layer.
code: IPD trials            AgD trials                 |                      |    [adjustment layer: PAIC]           |    cstc() / cmaic() / cmlnmr()        |    -> effect-modifier-adjusted        |       contrasts per edge              |                 \                    /                  v                  v         [connection layer: component NMA]         cnma_bridge()  (netmeta::discomb)         component effects beta; theta = C %*% beta                          |                          v         connected + population-adjusted network         relative_effects(), league_table(), component_effects()

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: Phase 1 (frequentist core) — cnma_bridge(), cmaic(), cstc(). Phase 2 (Bayesian flagship) — cmlnmr(): component-additive ML-NMR (treatment effect = C %*% beta_component), integrating individual model study’s covariate distribution. Disconnected sub-networks share component parameters therefore connected construction. Status: four families implemented validated via cmdstanr — inst/stan/cpaic_binomial.stan (logit), cpaic_normal.stan (identity; exact covariate means), cpaic_poisson.stan (log), cpaic_survival.stan (piecewise-exponential proportional hazards: step-function baseline log-hazard one level per cut_points interval; cut_points = NULL exponential, intervals give flexible baseline). IPD Lexis-expanded internally; aggregate survival events + person-time per arm per interval. Caveat: components effect modifiers informed aggregate data weakly identified (prior_reg_sd regularizes); aggregate survival likelihood uses person-time times integrated- hazard approximation, accurate narrow intervals. Phase 3 — validation (simulation + reproduction published examples) documentation suite.

what: Important: STC is written natively here
title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: mlumr::stc() unanchored (single index-vs-comparator pair, common comparator). cpaic targets anchored/connected comparisons, cstc() implemented -package: fit outcome regression IPD treatment + covariate main effects + treatment×effect-modifier interactions, predict relative effect comparator (AgD) population, anchor (Bucher) AgD trial’s within-trial contrast. delta-method link machinery adapted mlumr’s stc.R/link_functions.R attribution. mlumr Suggests, Imports.

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
code: R/   cpaic-package.R    # _PACKAGE doc, namespace imports   data_setup.R       # cpaic_network(): wrap multinma data model + component coding   connectivity.R     # cpaic_connectivity(): disconnection + identifiability (rank X = c)   bridge.R           # cnma_bridge(): discomb-based reconstruction   cmaic.R            # cmaic(): per-edge maicplus weights -> bridge   cstc.R             # cstc(): native anchored STC -> bridge   cmlnmr.R           # Phase 2: component-additive ML-NMR   effects.R          # relative_effects/component_effects/league_table + S3   diagnostics.R      # additivity Q-test, ESS, integration checks   plot.R             # component network plots, forest plots   utils.R, zzz.R inst/stan/           # Phase 2 component-additive ML-NMR models (compiled via cmdstanr at runtime) tests/testthat/ data/, data-raw/     # example datasets (incl. constructed disconnected+IPD example) vignettes/           # user-facing .Rmd documentation/       # GITIGNORED. Technical docs, refs, validation, progress log.

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: four: binary (/RR/RD), continuous/normal (MD), count/ Poisson (RR), survival/TTE (HR, RMST). Note maicplus natively covers binary + TTE, cmaic() continuous/count uses weighted-GLM path directly rather maic_anchored().

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: Quarto math doc: quarto render documentation/mathematical-foundations/cpaic-math.qmd.
code: devtools::load_all()                 # load package devtools::document()                 # roxygen -> NAMESPACE + man/ devtools::test()                     # run testthat devtools::check()                    # R CMD check pkgdown::build_site()                # docs site # Phase 2 Stan models compile lazily with cmdstanr; cache under tools::R_user_dir.

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: roxygen2 (markdown), testthat edition 3, S3 classes (cpaic_network, cpaic_fit, cpaic_bridge, …) mirroring multinma/mlumr style. Distribution: CRAN-eligible (four engine deps CRAN). American English spelling; dash punctuation connector (see user’s global style rules). GPL-3.

title: CLAUDE.md — cpaic developer reference
text: Rücker 2020/2021 — additive cNMA; cNMA vs matching disconnected network (conceptual seed; code appendices). Wigle & Béliveau 2022 — unanchored additive cNMA (data-driven anchor; anchor-misspecification bias). Efthimiou 2022 — Bayesian AD+IPD cNMA component×covariate interactions (closest prior art adjustment layer). Veroniki 2026 — guidance/decision tree cNMA disconnected networks (additivity-across-subnetworks assumption). Phillippo et al. 2020 — ML-NMR (basis cmlnmr()).

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: Network meta-analysis needs connected network. evidence splits two sub-networks common comparator, network disconnected treatments different sub-networks compared directly. cpaic reconnects network additive component structure treatments (component network meta-analysis), adjusts comparisons -study differences effect modifiers using anchored population-adjustment methods (STC, MAIC, ML-NMR). result indirect comparison connected population-adjusted.

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: bundled data describe binary-outcome network two pieces: sub-network 1, anchored placebo: Placebo, , B; sub-network 2, isolated: +B, +B+C, +B+D. treatment shared two pieces, network disconnected. shared components B bridge .
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo") net #> cpaic component network #>   Summary measure:   OR #>   Treatments:        6 #>   Components:        4 (A, B, C, D) #>   AgD comparisons:   5 #>   Reference:         Placebo #>   Inactive:          Placebo #>   IPD studies:       2 (binomial; 3200 patients) #>   Connected:         FALSE | components bridgeable: TRUE

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: disconnected network can bridged shared components make component effects identifiable, rank(X) = number components. report confirms two sub-networks, identifies B bridging components, shows component effects identifiable.
code: cpaic_connectivity(net) #> cpaic connectivity #>   Connected network: FALSE #>   Sub-networks:      2 #>     [1] 3 treatments #>     [2] 3 treatments #>   Bridging components: A, B #>   Component identifiability: rank(X) = 4 / 4 components -> IDENTIFIABLE plot(net)

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: cnma_bridge() fits additive component model reconstructs relative effects across gap.

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: Components C D come IPD studies, whose effect modifier x1 imbalanced relative target population (x1 = 0). Anchored STC fits outcome regression treatment--x1 interactions reads treatment effect target. Anchored MAIC instead reweights IPD study target population. Population adjustment moves C D effects relative unadjusted (naive) bridge, placebo-anchored components B unchanged:
code: fit_stc <- cstc(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1") component_effects(fit_stc) #>   component  estimate        se        lower     upper statistic        pval #> 1         A 0.5000000 0.2563324 -0.002402322 1.0024023  1.950592 0.051105590 #> 2         B 0.4000000 0.2563324 -0.102402322 0.9024023  1.560474 0.118647988 #> 3         C 0.4896667 0.2406290  0.018042458 0.9612910  2.034944 0.041856471 #> 4         D 0.6408956 0.2317142  0.186744196 1.0950470  2.765889 0.005676788 fit_maic <- cmaic(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1",                   n_boot = 100, seed = 1) effective_sample_size(fit_maic) #>       S3       S4  #> 207.4202 358.1461 data.frame(   component = component_effects(br)$component,   naive     = round(component_effects(br)$estimate, 3),   cSTC      = round(component_effects(fit_stc)$estimate, 3),   cMAIC     = round(component_effects(fit_maic)$estimate, 3) ) #>   component naive  cSTC cMAIC #> 1         A 0.500 0.500 0.500 #> 2         B 0.400 0.400 0.400 #> 3         C 0.717 0.490 0.697 #> 4         D 0.325 0.641 0.772

title: Getting started with cpaic
code: relative_effects(fit_stc) #> Relative effects (OR, back-transformed) #>  treatment comparator estimate    se lower  upper     z     p #>          A    Placebo    1.649 0.256 0.998  2.725 1.951 0.051 #>        A+B    Placebo    2.460 0.363 1.209  5.005 2.483 0.013 #>      A+B+C    Placebo    4.014 0.435 1.711  9.416 3.194 0.001 #>      A+B+D    Placebo    4.669 0.430 2.009 10.850 3.582 0.000 #>          B    Placebo    1.492 0.256 0.903  2.466 1.560 0.119 additivity_test(fit_stc) #> Additivity (Cochran Q) test of the component model #>   Q = 2.669, df = 1, p = 0.102 forest(fit_stc)

title: Getting started with cpaic
text: vignette("cpaic-methods") covers statistical framework depth. full mathematical foundations validation study provided development sources.

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: cpaic targets networks disconnected: treatments split two sub-networks common comparator, standard network meta-analysis compare across gap. Two layers solve two distinct problems. Connection layer (component network meta-analysis). Multi-component treatments decomposed additive component effects. sub-networks share components, component effects bridge gap. Adjustment layer (population adjustment). individual patient data (IPD) available, evidence edge corrected -study imbalance effect modifiers, using anchored STC, MAIC, ML-NMR. output indirect comparison connected population-adjusted.

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: Let δ\delta vector observed relative effects (one per comparison), BB edge-incidence (contrast) matrix mapping comparisons treatments, CC treatment--component matrix Ctc=1C_{tc} = 1 treatment tt contains component cc. Treatment effects additive component effects β\beta, θ=Cβ,δ=Bθ=BCβ=Xβ, \theta = C\beta, \qquad \delta = B\theta = BC\beta = X\beta,  X=BCX = BC component design matrix. inverse-variance weights WW, component effects estimated weighted least squares β̂=(X⊤WX)+X⊤Wd,Cov(θ̂)=C(X⊤WX)+C⊤, \hat\beta = (X^\top W X)^{+} X^\top W d, \qquad    \mathrm{Cov}(\hat\theta) = C (X^\top W X)^{+} C^\top,  (⋅)+(\cdot)^{+} Moore-Penrose inverse dd data vector (Rücker et al. 2020). additivity assumption checked Cochran QQ statistic. implemented cnma_bridge(), wrapper around netmeta::discomb().

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: disconnected network can bridged shared components make component effects identifiable, rank(X)=K\mathrm{rank}(X) = K, number components. cpaic_connectivity() detects sub-networks, lists bridging components, reports identifiability. identifiable, cnma_bridge() reconstructs relative effects across gap component effects.
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, sm = "OR", inactive = "Placebo") cpaic_connectivity(net) #> cpaic connectivity #>   Connected network: FALSE #>   Sub-networks:      2 #>     [1] 3 treatments #>     [2] 3 treatments #>   Bridging components: A, B #>   Component identifiability: rank(X) = 4 / 4 components -> IDENTIFIABLE component_effects(cnma_bridge(net)) #>   component  estimate        se     lower    upper statistic      pval #> 1         A 0.5000000 1.1922140 -1.836697 2.836697 0.4193878 0.6749328 #> 2         B 0.4000000 1.1922140 -1.936697 2.736697 0.3355102 0.7372402 #> 3         C 0.7170248 0.9734562 -1.190914 2.624964 0.7365763 0.4613800 #> 4         D 0.3250136 0.9728622 -1.581761 2.231788 0.3340798 0.7383193

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: IPD study, cstc() fits outcome regression treatment main effects, prognostic main effects, treatment--effect-modifier interactions, effect modifiers centered target population. link scale, g{E(y∣arm t,x)}=μ+βt+γt⊤(x−x‾target)+… g\{E(y \mid \text{arm } t, x)\}    = \mu + \beta_t + \gamma_t^\top (x - \bar x_{\text{target}}) + \dots  treatment coefficient βt\beta_t population-adjusted contrast target (interaction term vanishes x=x‾targetx = \bar x_{\text{target}}). anchored generalization regression-based standardization; implemented natively mlumr::stc() targets unanchored two-trial case.

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: cmaic() reweights IPD study effect-modifier distribution matches target population, using entropy-balancing weights wi=exp⁡(x̃⊤α)w_i = \exp(\tilde x_i^\top \alpha) x̃\tilde x_i centered effect modifiers (Phillippo et al. 2020). effective sample size ESS=(∑iwi)2/∑iwi2\mathrm{ESS} = (\sum_i w_i)^2 / \sum_i w_i^2. weighted within-study contrasts, bootstrap standard errors propagate weighting uncertainty, combined component model.
code: fit_maic <- cmaic(net_ipd, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1",                   n_boot = 100, seed = 1) effective_sample_size(fit_maic) #>       S3       S4  #> 207.4202 358.1461

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: single IPD edge anchored common comparator, combining adjusted contrast aggregate comparator contrast component model exactly Bucher indirect comparison. component model generalizes network: many adjusted unadjusted contrasts combined simultaneously β̂=(X⊤WX)+X⊤Wd\hat\beta = (X^\top W X)^{+} X^\top W d, bridge supplies contrasts otherwise unavailable across disconnection. key assumption component effects (effect-modifier interactions) constant across sub-networks (Rücker et al. 2021).

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: cmlnmr() places component structure inside multilevel network meta-regression: relative effect arm CβC\beta rather free per-treatment parameter, aggregate arms fitted integrating individual model study’s covariate distribution. Disconnected sub-networks share component parameters, network connected construction. four outcome families supported; survival uses proportional-hazards model flexible baseline (piecewise-exponential default, smooth M-spline), set cut_points baseline. Aggregate covariates integrated Gaussian copula whose correlation estimated individual patient data. component whose effect-modifier interaction informed aggregate data weakly identified relies regression prior.
code: # Requires cmdstanr; see ?cmlnmr. fit <- cmlnmr(ipd, agd, effect_modifiers = "x1", inactive = "Placebo",               family = "binomial") component_effects(fit)

title: The cpaic statistical framework
text: Additivity component effects (test additivity_test(); add interaction terms violated). Identifiability: disconnected network bridgeable cpaic_connectivity() reports identifiable = TRUE. Cross-population transportability effect modifiers (standard population-adjustment assumption), extended constancy component effects across sub-networks. Non-collapsibility: MAIC targets marginal effect STC conditional effect, noncollapsible measures answer slightly different questions.

code: @Manual{,   title = {cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison},   author = {Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/choxos/cpaic}, }

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison cpaic extends component network meta-analysis (cNMA) population-adjusted indirect comparison (PAIC) methods, disconnected treatment network can reconnected shared treatment components , time, adjusted -study differences effect modifiers. Standard network meta-analysis needs connected network. network disconnected (common comparator linking two sub-networks), analyzed directly. Two ideas solve part problem: Component NMA (Rücker et al. 2020) decomposes multi-component treatments additive component effects; sub-networks share components, component effects bridge gap — aggregate data , ignoring effect-modifier imbalance. PAIC (STC, MAIC, ML-NMR) adjusts effect-modifier imbalance using individual patient data (IPD), assumes connected/anchored network. cpaic combines : component structure provides bridge, anchored STC / MAIC / ML-NMR provide population adjustment.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Frequentist core — available. cnma_bridge() (reconnect via components), cmaic() (component MAIC), cstc() (component anchored STC), binary, continuous, count, survival outcomes. Bayesian flagship cmlnmr() (component-additive ML-NMR) — available. Binary, continuous, count, survival outcomes; fitted cmdstanr. Survival uses piecewise-exponential proportional-hazards model flexible (step-function) baseline set cut_points.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: cpaic builds netmeta (cNMA), maicplus (MAIC weights), multinma (ML-NMR / numerical integration), CRAN.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("choxos/cpaic")

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: relative_effects(), league_table(), component_effects(), additivity_test(), effective_sample_size(), forest() plot() summarize visualize fit.
code: library(cpaic)  # A disconnected network: sub-network {Placebo, A, B} and isolated # sub-network {A+B, A+B+C, A+B+D}, bridged by the shared components A and B. net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo")  # Is it bridgeable? (disconnected, but components make it identifiable) cpaic_connectivity(net)  # 1. Connect only (aggregate component NMA) cnma_bridge(net)  # 2. Connect + population-adjust via anchored STC cstc(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1")  # 3. Connect + population-adjust via anchored MAIC cmaic(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1")

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Technical documentation (mathematical foundations, methods manual, validation study) lives local documentation/ folder. User-facing vignettes installed package.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: Rücker G, Petropoulou M, Schwarzer G (2020). Network meta-analysis multicomponent interventions. Biometrical Journal 62(3):808–821. Phillippo DM, et al. (2020). Multilevel network meta-regression population-adjusted treatment comparisons. JRSS 183(3):1189–1210.

title: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison
text: GPL-3

what: Test the additivity assumption of the component model — additivity_test
title: Test the additivity assumption of the component model — additivity_test
text: Returns Cochran Q statistic additive component network meta-analysis (netmeta::discomb()). small p-value indicates lack fit additive model, .e. evidence component interactions; consider adding interaction terms.

title: Test the additivity assumption of the component model — additivity_test
code: additivity_test(object, ...)

title: Test the additivity assumption of the component model — additivity_test
text: object cpaic_bridge cpaic_fit object. ... Unused.

title: Test the additivity assumption of the component model — additivity_test
text: one-row data frame Q, df, pval.

what: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
text: Splits multi-component treatment labels (e.g. "+ B") sep.comps returns binary treatment--component design matrix C, C[t, c] = 1 treatment t contains component c. inactive component (e.g. placebo) represented -zero row, matching convention netmeta::netcomb().

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
code: build_C_matrix(treatments, sep.comps = "+", inactive = NULL)

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
text: treatments Character vector (unique) treatment labels. sep.comps Single character separating components treatment label. Default "+". inactive Optional name inactive/reference treatment component (mapped zero row / dropped column).

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
text: binary matrix one row per treatment one column per component (treatments row names, components column names).

title: Build a component-coded treatment-by-component matrix — build_C_matrix
code: build_C_matrix(c("A", "B", "A + B", "placebo"), inactive = "placebo") #>         A B #> A       1 0 #> B       0 1 #> A + B   1 1 #> placebo 0 0

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
text: Anchored MAIC generalized (possibly disconnected) component network. IPD study reweighted maicplus::estimate_weights() effect-modifier distribution matches common target population; resulting population-adjusted within-study contrasts (bootstrap standard errors propagate weighting uncertainty) replace corresponding unadjusted aggregate contrasts. Finally cnma_bridge() combines contrasts additive component model, yielding relative effects connected across sub-networks adjusted target population.

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
code: cmaic(   network,   target,   effect_modifiers = NULL,   target_sd = NULL,   n_boot = 500,   seed = NULL,   common = FALSE,   random = TRUE )

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
text: network cpaic_network() object includes IPD. target Named numeric vector (one-row data frame / list) giving target-population means effect modifiers. effect_modifiers Character vector covariates match (defaults IPD covariates). Matching effect modifiers anchored-MAIC convention. target_sd Optional named numeric vector target standard deviations; supplied, second moments matched well. n_boot Number bootstrap resamples adjusted-contrast standard errors. Default 500. seed Optional RNG seed reproducible bootstrap. common, random Passed cnma_bridge().

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
text: object class cpaic_maic (also inheriting cpaic_bridge structure via $bridge), bridged fit, per-study effective sample sizes, target population.

title: Component matching-adjusted indirect comparison (cMAIC) — cmaic
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo") # \donttest{ fit <- cmaic(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1",              n_boot = 100, seed = 1) relative_effects(fit) #> Relative effects (OR, back-transformed) #>  treatment comparator estimate    se lower  upper     z     p #>          A    Placebo    1.649 0.401 0.752  3.615 1.248 0.212 #>        A+B    Placebo    2.460 0.567 0.810  7.466 1.589 0.112 #>      A+B+C    Placebo    4.941 0.672 1.323 18.448 2.377 0.017 #>      A+B+D    Placebo    5.324 0.666 1.443 19.647 2.510 0.012 #>          B    Placebo    1.492 0.401 0.680  3.271 0.999 0.318 effective_sample_size(fit) #>       S3       S4  #> 207.4202 358.1461  # }

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: Bayesian flagship cpaic. relative effect every treatment sum component effects (theta = C beta), estimated jointly individual patient data (IPD) aggregate data (AgD). Aggregate arms fitted integrating individual-level model study's covariate distribution. disconnected sub-networks share component parameters, network connected construction.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
code: cmlnmr(   ipd,   agd,   effect_modifiers,   inactive = NULL,   sep.comps = "+",   family = "binomial",   study = ".study",   trt = ".trt",   outcome = ".y",   time = ".time",   exposure = ".exposure",   r = "r",   n = "n",   E = "E",   se = "se",   cut_points = NULL,   interval = ".interval",   baseline = c("piecewise", "mspline"),   n_basis = 6L,   cor = NULL,   n_int = 64L,   prior_intercept_sd = 10,   prior_beta_sd = 10,   prior_reg_sd = 2.5,   chains = 4L,   iter_warmup = 500L,   iter_sampling = 500L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: ipd Individual patient data (one row per patient). agd Aggregate data (one row per arm) per-study covariate summaries x_mean, x_sd effect modifier x. effect_modifiers Character vector effect-modifier names. inactive, sep.comps Component coding (see cpaic_network()). family One "binomial", "gaussian", "poisson", "survival". study, trt Column names (ipd agd). outcome IPD outcome column: 0/1 (binomial), numeric (gaussian), count (poisson), event indicator survival. time, exposure IPD time / exposure column (survival, poisson). r, n, E, se Aggregate columns: events r, sample size n (binomial), exposure E (poisson/survival), mean outcome standard error se (gaussian). cut_points Survival : interior interval boundaries piecewise-exponential baseline. NULL (default) gives exponential model; e.g. c(6, 12) gives three intervals. interval Survival : name aggregate interval-index column (values 1..K) required cut_points supplied. baseline Survival baseline hazard: "piecewise" (default, step function) "mspline" (smooth M-spline cut_points grid). n_basis Number M-spline basis functions baseline = "mspline" (must <= number intervals). cor Optional covariate correlation matrix Gaussian-copula integration. Defaults IPD correlation; pass matrix override identity integrate covariates independently. n_int Integration points per aggregate arm (ignored gaussian, exact covariate means). prior_intercept_sd, prior_beta_sd, prior_reg_sd Prior SDs. chains, iter_warmup, iter_sampling, seed Passed cmdstanr. ... Reserved.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: object class cpaic_mlnmr cmdstanr fit, component design, tidy table component effects.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
text: Supported families: "binomial" (logit), "gaussian" (identity), "poisson" (log), "survival". Survival uses proportional-hazards model flexible baseline: baseline = "piecewise" gives piecewise-exponential step baseline (one level per interval defined cut_points; exponential cut_points = NULL), baseline = "mspline" gives smooth baseline hazard non-negative M-spline basis (n_basis functions, simplex weights) evaluated interval midpoints cut_points grid. Individual patient data split cut points internally; aggregate survival data supplied events person-time per arm (cut_points given) per interval. aggregate likelihood approximates expected events arm-interval person-time times integrated hazard; assumes person-time independent covariates within interval (accurate intervals narrow), approximate effect modifiers also drive censoring. Aggregate covariates integrated Gaussian copula: correlation estimated IPD (supplied via cor) integration points respect covariate correlation structure rather treating effect modifiers independent. Identifiability note: component whose effect-modifier interaction informed aggregate arms weakly identified, main interaction effects constrained integrated population-average outcome. Supply IPD components possible; prior_reg_sd regularizes otherwise.

title: Component-additive multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR) — cmlnmr
code: if (FALSE) { # requireNamespace("cmdstanr", quietly = TRUE) && !inherits(try(cmdstanr::cmdstan_path(), silent = TRUE), "try-error") # \donttest{ ipd <- data.frame(.study = "S1",                   .trt = rep(c("Placebo", "A"), each = 100),                   .y = rbinom(200, 1, 0.5), x1 = rnorm(200)) agd <- data.frame(.study = "S2", .trt = c("Placebo", "A+B"),                   r = c(40, 55), n = c(100, 100),                   x1_mean = c(0.2, 0.2), x1_sd = c(1, 1)) fit <- cmlnmr(ipd, agd, effect_modifiers = "x1", inactive = "Placebo",               chains = 2, iter_warmup = 200, iter_sampling = 200) component_effects(fit) # } }

what: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: Fits additive component network meta-analysis (cNMA) model Rücker et al. (2020) aggregate contrast data, using netmeta::discomb(). network disconnected sub-networks share components, additive model estimates component effects reconstructs (otherwise unavailable) relative effects across sub-networks. "connect first" step; population adjustment layered cmaic() / cstc(), replace unadjusted contrasts adjusted ones calling function.

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
code: cnma_bridge(network, common = FALSE, random = TRUE, ...)

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: network cpaic_network() object. common, random Fit common- /random-effects models. ... Additional arguments passed netmeta::discomb() (e.g. tau.preset).

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: object class cpaic_bridge wrapping netmeta::discomb() fit, tidied component treatment effects.

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge
text: Rücker G, Petropoulou M, Schwarzer G (2020). Network meta-analysis multicomponent interventions. Biometrical Journal, 62(3), 808–821.

title: Reconnect a network through its additive component structure — cnma_bridge

what: cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison — cpaic-package
title: cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison — cpaic-package
text: Extends component network meta-analysis (cNMA) population-adjusted indirect comparison (PAIC) methods disconnected treatment networks can reconnected adjusted -study differences effect modifiers. additive component structure cNMA (Ruecker et al. 2020 doi:10.1002/bimj.201800167 ) used bridge otherwise disconnected sub-networks share treatment components, anchored simulated treatment comparison (STC), matching-adjusted indirect comparison (MAIC), multilevel network meta-regression (ML-NMR; Phillippo et al. 2020 doi:10.1111/rssa.12579 ) provide population adjustment using individual patient data. Supports binary, continuous, count, time--event outcomes. Builds 'netmeta', 'maicplus', 'multinma' packages.

title: cpaic: Component-Based Population-Adjusted Indirect Comparison — cpaic-package
text: Maintainer: Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi ahmad.pub@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Ahmad Sofi-Mahmudi ahmad.pub@gmail.com (ORCID)

what: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
text: constructed binary-outcome network used throughout examples tests. disconnected: sub-network 1 (Placebo, , B) anchored placebo, sub-network 2 (+B, +B+C, +B+D) shares treatment sub-network 1. shared components B bridge two, components C D identified within sub-network 2.

title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
code: cpaic_bin_agd

title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
text: data frame 5 rows 5 columns: studlab Study label. treat1, treat2 Treatments compared (components joined "+"). TE Log odds ratio treat1 versus treat2. seTE Standard error TE. attribute "truth" holds data-generating component log-odds ratios.

title: Example disconnected component network: aggregate contrasts — cpaic_bin_agd
text: Studies S3 S4 also individual patient data (cpaic_bin_ipd); rows unadjusted contrasts, cmaic() / cstc() replace population-adjusted versions.

what: Example disconnected component network: individual patient data — cpaic_bin_ipd
title: Example disconnected component network: individual patient data — cpaic_bin_ipd
text: Individual patient data studies S3 (+B+C vs +B) S4 (+B+D vs +B) cpaic_bin_agd network. single effect modifier x1 imbalanced relative target population (x1 = 0), population adjustment changes C D component effects.

title: Example disconnected component network: individual patient data — cpaic_bin_ipd
code: cpaic_bin_ipd

title: Example disconnected component network: individual patient data — cpaic_bin_ipd
text: data frame 3200 rows 4 columns: .study Study label (S3 S4). .trt Treatment arm. .y Binary outcome (0/1). x1 Continuous effect modifier. attribute "truth" holds data-generating parameters.

what: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: Reports whether treatment network connected, – crucially disconnected network – whether additive component structure makes component effects estimable. disconnected network bridgeable sub-networks share enough components component design matrix X = B C full column rank (rank(X) = number components). Otherwise component effects uniquely identified bridge produce arbitrary estimates.

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
code: cpaic_connectivity(network, tol = 1e-08)

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: network cpaic_network() object. tol Numerical tolerance rank computation.

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
text: object class cpaic_connectivity: list connected (logical), n_subnetworks, subnetworks (list treatment-label vectors), bridging_components (components shared across sub-networks), rank n_components, identifiable (logical: rank == n_components), B/C/X matrices.

title: Assess connectivity and component-bridge identifiability of a network — cpaic_connectivity
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, sm = "OR", inactive = "Placebo") cpaic_connectivity(net) #> cpaic connectivity #>   Connected network: FALSE #>   Sub-networks:      2 #>     [1] 3 treatments #>     [2] 3 treatments #>   Bridging components: A, B #>   Component identifiability: rank(X) = 4 / 4 components -> IDENTIFIABLE

what: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: Builds network object consumed cnma_bridge(), cmaic(), cstc() (Phase 2) cmlnmr(). Aggregate data supplied contrast level (one row per pairwise comparison: treat1 vs treat2, treatment effect TE standard error seTE), matching input convention netmeta::discomb(). Individual patient data (IPD) optional used population-adjusted methods replace study's unadjusted contrast adjusted one.

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
code: cpaic_network(   agd,   ipd = NULL,   treat1 = "treat1",   treat2 = "treat2",   TE = "TE",   seTE = "seTE",   studlab = "studlab",   sm,   inactive = NULL,   sep.comps = "+",   reference = NULL,   family = NULL,   ipd_study = ".study",   ipd_trt = ".trt",   ipd_outcome = ".y",   ipd_time = NULL,   ipd_status = NULL,   ipd_exposure = NULL,   ipd_covariates = NULL )

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: agd Aggregate (contrast-level) data frame. ipd Optional individual patient data frame (one row per patient). Must contain study column, treatment column, outcome, effect-modifier / prognostic covariates. treat1, treat2, TE, seTE, studlab Column names agd. sm Summary measure (e.g. "", "RR", "MD", "HR"), passed netmeta::discomb(). inactive Name inactive component / reference (e.g. "placebo"). sep.comps Component separator treatment labels. Default "+". reference Reference treatment reported relative effects. Defaults inactive available, otherwise first treatment. family Outcome family IPD model, one "binomial", "gaussian", "poisson", "survival" (required ipd given). ipd_study, ipd_trt, ipd_outcome Column names ipd. survival outcomes use ipd_time/ipd_status instead (alongside) ipd_outcome; Poisson rates optional ipd_exposure offset. ipd_time, ipd_status Time event-indicator column names ipd (survival family). ipd_exposure Optional exposure/person-time column ipd (Poisson family); used log offset. ipd_covariates Character vector covariate column names ipd (candidate effect modifiers / prognostic factors).

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: object class cpaic_network: list elements agd, ipd, treatments, components, C.matrix, sm, inactive, reference, sep.comps, family, column-name mappings.

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
text: Treatment labels encode components via sep.comps (e.g. "+ B"), sub-networks sharing components can bridged.

title: Set up a (possibly disconnected) component network for cpaic — cpaic_network
code: # Aggregate-only, disconnected network net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, sm = "OR", inactive = "Placebo") net #> cpaic component network #>   Summary measure:   OR #>   Treatments:        6 #>   Components:        4 (A, B, C, D) #>   AgD comparisons:   5 #>   Reference:         Placebo #>   Inactive:          Placebo #>   IPD studies:        none #>   Connected:         FALSE | components bridgeable: TRUE  # With individual patient data for the population-adjusted methods net_ipd <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                          family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                          inactive = "Placebo")

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: Anchored STC generalized (possibly disconnected) component network. IPD study outcome regression fitted treatment main effects, prognostic main effects, treatment--effect-modifier interactions; effect modifiers centered common target population treatment coefficient anchored, population-adjusted contrast population. adjusted contrasts replace corresponding unadjusted aggregate contrasts cnma_bridge() combines additive component model.

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
code: cstc(   network,   target,   effect_modifiers = NULL,   prognostics = NULL,   common = FALSE,   random = TRUE )

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: network cpaic_network() object includes IPD. target Named numeric vector (list / one-row data frame) target-population means effect modifiers. effect_modifiers Covariates interact treatment (centered target). Defaults IPD covariates. prognostics Covariates included main effects . Defaults effect modifiers (enters main effect + interaction). common, random Passed cnma_bridge().

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: object class cpaic_stc (cpaic_fit).

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
text: Unlike cmaic() (reweighting) regression-adjustment route. reported treatment coefficient conditional effect target effect-modifier means (marginal standardization); collapsible measures two coincide. implemented natively mlumr::stc() targets unanchored two-trial case; link standard-error machinery adapted package.

title: Component simulated treatment comparison (cSTC) — cstc
code: net <- cpaic_network(cpaic_bin_agd, ipd = cpaic_bin_ipd, sm = "OR",                      family = "binomial", ipd_covariates = "x1",                      inactive = "Placebo") fit <- cstc(net, target = c(x1 = 0), effect_modifiers = "x1") relative_effects(fit) #> Relative effects (OR, back-transformed) #>  treatment comparator estimate    se lower  upper     z     p #>          A    Placebo    1.649 0.256 0.998  2.725 1.951 0.051 #>        A+B    Placebo    2.460 0.363 1.209  5.005 2.483 0.013 #>      A+B+C    Placebo    4.014 0.435 1.711  9.416 3.194 0.001 #>      A+B+D    Placebo    4.669 0.430 2.009 10.850 3.582 0.000 #>          B    Placebo    1.492 0.256 0.903  2.466 1.560 0.119 additivity_test(fit) #> Additivity (Cochran Q) test of the component model #>   Q = 2.669, df = 1, p = 0.102

what: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
title: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
text: Tidies (random- common-effects) relative effects component-bridged model: every treatment versus chosen reference, pairwise comparisons. Effects reported natural scale summary measure (e.g. odds ratios) unless backtransf = FALSE.

title: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
code: relative_effects(   object,   reference = NULL,   all_contrasts = FALSE,   backtransf = TRUE,   level = 0.95,   ... )

title: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
text: object fitted cpaic object (cpaic_bridge, cpaic_maic, cpaic_stc, cpaic_mlnmr). reference Reference treatment. Defaults network reference. all_contrasts TRUE, return pairwise comparisons instead versus reference. backtransf TRUE (default) back-transform log-scale measures (/RR/HR/...) exponentiating. level Confidence level intervals. Default 0.95. ... Unused.

title: Relative treatment effects from a cpaic fit — relative_effects
text: data frame columns treatment, comparator, estimate, se (link scale), lower, upper, z/p frequentist fits. cmlnmr() (Bayesian) fits intervals credible intervals final column pr_gt0, posterior probability effect (link scale) exceeds zero, instead z/p.
